session.h(3) Library Functions Manual session.h(3)

NAME

session.h

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct ring_consensus_t
Ring consensus state (Host-Mediated Proactive Election). struct host_liveness_t
Host liveness detection state. struct migration_ctx_t
Host migration context for tracking failover state. struct discovery_session_t
Discovery session context. struct discovery_config_t
Configuration for discovery session.

Macros


#define MAX_PARTICIPANTS 16

Typedefs


typedef bool(* discovery_should_exit_fn) (void *user_data)
Callback to check if session should exit.

Enumerations


enum migration_state_t { MIGRATION_STATE_NONE, MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED, MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER, MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE }
Migration detection and failover state. enum discovery_state_t { DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_ACDS, DISCOVERY_STATE_CREATING_SESSION, DISCOVERY_STATE_JOINING_SESSION, DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER, DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING, DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE, DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED, DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED }
Discovery session state.

Functions


discovery_session_t * discovery_session_create (const discovery_config_t *config)
Create a new discovery session. void discovery_session_destroy (discovery_session_t *session)
Destroy discovery session and free resources. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start (discovery_session_t *session)
Start the discovery session. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process (discovery_session_t *session, int64_t timeout_ns)
Process session events (call in main loop). void discovery_session_stop (discovery_session_t *session)
Stop the discovery session. discovery_state_t discovery_session_get_state (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get current session state. bool discovery_session_is_active (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if session is active (call in progress). const char * discovery_session_get_string (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get session string. bool discovery_session_is_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the host. session_host_t * discovery_session_get_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Get host context (if we are host). session_participant_t * discovery_session_get_participant (discovery_session_t *session)
Get participant context (if we are participant). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_init_ring (discovery_session_t *session)
Initialize ring consensus state. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start_ring_round (discovery_session_t *session)
Start a new ring consensus round (every 5 minutes or on new joiner). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive (discovery_session_t *session)
Detect host disconnect (check connection status). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect (discovery_session_t *session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)
Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_become_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Become the host (called when elected as future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_connect_to_future_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Connect to pre-elected future host (called when NOT future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_get_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session, uint8_t out_id[16], char out_address[64], uint16_t *out_port, uint8_t *out_connection_type)
Get future host information. bool discovery_session_is_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the future host.

Definition at line 36 of file src/discovery/session.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef bool(* discovery_should_exit_fn) (void *user_data)

Callback to check if session should exit. Called periodically during blocking operations (e.g., ACDS connect, join). Should return true to gracefully cancel the current operation.

Parameters

user_data Opaque pointer provided by caller

Returns

true to cancel operation, false to continue

Definition at line 114 of file src/discovery/session.h.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum discovery_state_t

Discovery session state.

Enumerator

DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT
Initial state.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_ACDS
Connecting to ACDS.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CREATING_SESSION
Creating new session.
DISCOVERY_STATE_JOINING_SESSION
Joining existing session.
DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER
Waiting for peer to join (initiator).
DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING
NAT negotiation in progress.
DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST
Starting as host.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST
Connecting to host as participant.
DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE
Session active (call in progress).
DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING
Host migration in progress.
DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED
Session failed.
DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED
Session ended.

enum migration_state_t


Migration detection and failover state. SIMPLIFIED DESIGN:
    No re-election during migration! Future host was pre-elected 5 minutes ago.
    When current host dies, we already know who takes over and where to find
    them.


Enumerator
  





  
MIGRATION_STATE_NONE
    

  
No migration in progress.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED
    

  
Host disconnect detected.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER
    

  
Failing over to pre-elected future host.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE
    

  
Failover complete, call resumed.

89   MIGRATION_STATE_NONE,     
90   MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED, 
91   MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER, 
92   MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE  
93 } migration_state_t;

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_become_host
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Become the host (called when elected as future host).


Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Definition at line 2297 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2297                                                                               {
2298   if (!session) {
2299     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2300     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2301   }
2302 
2303   log_info("Starting as new host after migration (participant ID: %02x%02x...)", session->participant_id[0],
2304            session->participant_id[1]);
2305 
2306   // Get configured port (or use default)
2307   int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
2308 
2309   // Mark ourselves as host
2310   session->is_host = true;
2311 
2312   // Create host context if needed with configured port
2313   if (!session->host_ctx) {
2314     session_host_config_t hconfig = {
2315         .port = host_port,
2316         .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
2317         .max_clients = 32,
2318         .encryption_enabled = true,
2319     };
2320 
2321     session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
2322     if (!session->host_ctx) {
2323       log_error("Failed to create host context for migration");
2324       return ERROR_MEMORY;
2325     }
2326 
2327     // Start listening for new participant connections
2328     asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
2329     if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2330       log_error("Failed to start host after migration: %d", hstart);
2331       return hstart;
2332     }
2333 
2334     log_info("Host restarted after migration, listening on port %d", host_port);
2335   }
2336 
2337   // Send HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS so other participants can reconnect
2338   acip_host_announcement_t announcement = {0};
2339   memcpy(announcement.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2340   memcpy(announcement.host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2341   SAFE_STRNCPY(announcement.host_address, session->host_address, sizeof(announcement.host_address));
2342   announcement.host_port = host_port;
2343   // Set connection type based on NAT quality (for now use DIRECT_PUBLIC for localhost dev environments)
2344   announcement.connection_type = ACIP_CONNECTION_TYPE_DIRECT_PUBLIC;
2345 
2346   if (session->acds_transport) {
2347     packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT, &announcement,
2348                               sizeof(announcement), 0);
2349     log_info("Sent HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS for new host %02x%02x... at %s:%u", announcement.host_id[0],
2350              announcement.host_id[1], announcement.host_address, announcement.host_port);
2351   }
2352 
2353   // Mark migration complete
2354   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2355 
2356   // Transition to ACTIVE state (host is ready)
2357   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2358 
2359   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2360 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Detect host disconnect (check connection status).


Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK if host alive, error if disconnected


Definition at line 2174 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2174                                                                                    {
2175   if (!session || session->is_host) {
2176     // We are the host, so "host" is always alive
2177     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2178   }
2179 
2180   // For WebRTC sessions, check the WebRTC transport instead of participant_ctx
2181   if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
2182     // If we have a WebRTC transport and it's marked as ready, host is alive
2183     if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
2184       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2185     }
2186     // WebRTC transport not ready yet - might still be connecting
2187     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2188   }
2189 
2190   // For TCP sessions, check participant context
2191   // If we don't have a participant context yet, we're not connected
2192   if (!session->participant_ctx) {
2193     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2194   }
2195 
2196   // Check migration state - if we're already migrating, host is definitely not alive
2197   if (session->migration.state != MIGRATION_STATE_NONE) {
2198     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Already detected disconnect
2199   }
2200 
2201   uint64_t now_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2202 
2203   // Check if we have a ping in flight that timed out
2204   if (session->liveness.ping_in_flight) {
2205     uint64_t ping_age_ms = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2206     if (ping_age_ms > session->liveness.timeout_ms) {
2207       // Ping timed out
2208       session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2209       session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
2210       log_warn("Host ping timeout (attempt %u/%u, age=%llu ms)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures,
2211                session->liveness.max_failures, (unsigned long long)ping_age_ms);
2212 
2213       // Check if we've exceeded failure threshold
2214       if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2215         log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive ping failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2216         return ERROR_NETWORK;
2217       }
2218     }
2219   }
2220 
2221   // Check if it's time to send a new ping
2222   uint64_t time_since_last_ping = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2223   if (!session->liveness.ping_in_flight && time_since_last_ping >= session->liveness.ping_interval_ms) {
2224     // Send ping to host via participant connection
2225     socket_t host_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
2226     if (host_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2227       asciichat_error_t result = packet_send(host_socket, PACKET_TYPE_PING, NULL, 0);
2228       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2229         session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = now_ms;
2230         session->liveness.ping_in_flight = true;
2231         log_debug("Sent ping to host (attempt %u/%u)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures + 1,
2232                   session->liveness.max_failures);
2233       } else {
2234         log_warn("Failed to send ping to host: %d", result);
2235         session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2236         if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2237           log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive send failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2238           return ERROR_NETWORK;
2239         }
2240       }
2241     }
2242   }
2243 
2244   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2245 }

2362                                                                                          {
2363   if (!session) {

2365     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2366   }
2367 
2368   log_info("Reconnecting to future host: %s:%u (connection type: %u)", session->ring.future_host_address,
2369            session->ring.future_host_port, session->ring.future_host_connection_type);
2370 
2371   // Destroy old participant context if present
2372   if (session->participant_ctx) {
2373     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);
2374     session->participant_ctx = NULL;
2375   }
2376 
2377   // Create new participant context for the future host
2378   session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
2379       .address = session->ring.future_host_address,
2380       .port = session->ring.future_host_port,
2381       .enable_audio = true,
2382       .enable_video = true,
2383       .encryption_enabled = true,
2384   };

2386   session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);

2395     log_error("Failed to connect to future host: %d", pconn);
2396     return pconn;
2397   }
2398 
2399   log_info("Connected to future host after migration");
2400 
2401   // Update our host info with the new future host details
2402   memcpy(session->host_id, session->ring.future_host_id, 16);
2403   SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->ring.future_host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
2404   session->host_port = session->ring.future_host_port;
2405 
2406   log_info("Updated host info to: %s:%u (id: %02x%02x...)", session->host_address, session->host_port,
2407            session->host_id[0], session->host_id[1]);
2408 
2409   // Mark migration complete (in real implementation, this would be done after successful connection)
2410   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2411 
2412   // Transition to ACTIVE state (participant is ready)
2413   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2414 
2415   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2416 }

Parameters


Config Module Session
  configuration


Returns


New session or NULL on error

50                                                                                 {
51   if (!config) {
52     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "config is NULL");
53     return NULL;
54   }
55 
56   discovery_session_t *session = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(discovery_session_t), discovery_session_t *);
57   if (!session) {
58     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate discovery session");
59     return NULL;
60   }
61 
62   session->state = DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT;
63   session->acds_transport = NULL;
64   session->peer_manager = NULL;
65   session->webrtc_transport_ready = false;
66   session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
67   session->webrtc_retry_attempt = 0;
68   session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = 0;
69   session->stun_servers = NULL;
70   session->stun_count = 0;
71   session->turn_servers = NULL;
72   session->turn_count = 0;
73 
74   // Initialize host liveness detection
75   session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = 0;
76   session->liveness.last_pong_received_ms = 0;
77   session->liveness.consecutive_failures = 0;
78   session->liveness.max_failures = 3;
79   session->liveness.ping_interval_ms = 3 * MS_PER_SEC_INT; // Ping every 3 seconds
80   session->liveness.timeout_ms = 10 * MS_PER_SEC_INT;      // 10 second timeout
81   session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
82 
83   log_info("discovery_session_create: After CALLOC - participant_ctx=%p, host_ctx=%p", session->participant_ctx,
84            session->host_ctx);
85 
86   // Load or generate identity key
87   char identity_path[256];
88   asciichat_error_t id_result = acds_identity_default_path(identity_path, sizeof(identity_path));
89   if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
90     id_result = acds_identity_load(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
91     if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
92       // File doesn't exist or is corrupted - generate new key
93       log_info("Identity key not found, generating new key");
94       id_result = acds_identity_generate(session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
95       if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
96         // Save for future use
97         acds_identity_save(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
98       }
99     }
100   }
101 
102   if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
103     log_warn("Failed to load/generate identity key, using zero key");
104     memset(session->identity_pubkey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_pubkey));
105     memset(session->identity_seckey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_seckey));
106   } else {
107     char fingerprint[65];
108     acds_identity_fingerprint(session->identity_pubkey, fingerprint);
109     log_info("Using identity key with fingerprint: %.16s...", fingerprint);
110   }
111 
112   // Load multiple identity keys from options (multi-key protocol support)
113   const options_t *opts = options_get();
114   if (opts && opts->num_identity_keys > 0) {
115     // Load all identity keys from options
116     for (size_t i = 0; i < opts->num_identity_keys && i < MAX_IDENTITY_KEYS; i++) {
117       uint8_t pubkey[32] = {0};
118       uint8_t seckey[64] = {0};
119       asciichat_error_t key_result = acds_identity_load(opts->identity_keys[i], pubkey, seckey);
120       if (key_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
121         memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[i], pubkey, 32);
122         char fingerprint[65];
123         acds_identity_fingerprint(pubkey, fingerprint);
124         log_info("Loaded identity key #%zu with fingerprint: %.16s...", i + 1, fingerprint);
125       } else {
126         log_warn("Failed to load identity key #%zu from %s", i + 1, opts->identity_keys[i]);
127       }
128     }
129     session->num_identity_keys = opts->num_identity_keys;
130     log_info("Loaded %zu identity key(s) for multi-key protocol", session->num_identity_keys);
131   } else {
132     // Single-key mode: use only the primary identity key
133     memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[0], session->identity_pubkey, 32);
134     session->num_identity_keys = 1;
135   }
136 
137   // ACDS connection info
138   if (config->acds_url && config->acds_url[0]) {
139     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_url, config->acds_url, sizeof(session->acds_url));
140   }
141   if (config->acds_address && config->acds_address[0]) {
142     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, config->acds_address, sizeof(session->acds_address));
143   } else {
144     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(session->acds_address));
145   }
146   session->acds_port = config->acds_port > 0 ? config->acds_port : ACIP_DISCOVERY_DEFAULT_PORT;
147 
148   // Session string (if joining)
149   if (config->session_string && config->session_string[0]) {
150     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->session_string, config->session_string, sizeof(session->session_string));
151     session->is_initiator = false;
152   } else {
153     session->is_initiator = true;
154   }
155 
156   // Callbacks
157   session->on_state_change = config->on_state_change;
158   session->on_session_ready = config->on_session_ready;
159   session->on_error = config->on_error;
160   session->callback_user_data = config->callback_user_data;
161   session->should_exit_callback = config->should_exit_callback;
162   session->exit_callback_data = config->exit_callback_data;
163 
164   // Initialize mutex for NAT quality protection
165   if (mutex_init(&session->nat_quality_mutex, "discovery_nat_quality") != 0) {
166     log_warn("Failed to initialize NAT quality mutex");
167   }
168 
169   log_debug("Discovery session created (initiator=%d, acds=%s:%u)", session->is_initiator, session->acds_address,
170             session->acds_port);
171 
172   return session;
173 }

177     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
178     return;
179   }
180 
181   // Close ACDS connection
182   if (session->acds_transport) {
183     acip_transport_destroy(session->acds_transport);
184     session->acds_transport = NULL;
185   }
186 
187   // Destroy host context if active
188   if (session->host_ctx) {
189     session_host_destroy(session->host_ctx);
190     session->host_ctx = NULL;
191   }
192 
193   // Destroy participant context if active
194   if (session->participant_ctx) {
195     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);
196     session->participant_ctx = NULL;
197   }
198 
199   // Clean up WebRTC peer manager (NEW)
200   if (session->peer_manager) {
201     webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(session->peer_manager);
202     session->peer_manager = NULL;
203   }
204 
205   // Clean up STUN/TURN server arrays (NEW)
206   if (session->stun_servers) {
207     SAFE_FREE(session->stun_servers);
208     session->stun_servers = NULL;
209     session->stun_count = 0;
210   }
211 
212   if (session->turn_servers) {
213     SAFE_FREE(session->turn_servers);
214     session->turn_servers = NULL;
215     session->turn_count = 0;
216   }
217 
218   // Wait for NAT detection thread to complete if still running (with 500ms timeout)
219   if (session->nat_detection_thread_started) {
220     log_debug("Waiting for NAT detection thread to complete (500ms timeout)...");
221     int join_result = asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&session->nat_detection_thread, NULL, 500 * 1000 * 1000); // 500ms
222     if (join_result != 0) {
223       log_warn("NAT detection thread did not exit within 500ms, continuing cleanup anyway");
224     }
225     session->nat_detection_thread_started = false;
226   }
227 
228   // Destroy NAT quality mutex
229   mutex_destroy(&session->nat_quality_mutex);
230 
231   SAFE_FREE(session);
232 }

  (discovery_session_t * session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)


Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host.


Parameters


Session Session context


disconnect_reason Reason code for disconnect


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on successful failover


Definition at line 2247 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2247                                                                                                                      {
2248   if (!session) {
2249     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2250     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2251   }
2252 
2253   log_warn("Host disconnect detected: reason=%u", disconnect_reason);
2254 
2255   // Initialize migration context
2256   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED;
2257   session->migration.detection_time_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2258   memcpy(session->migration.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2259   session->migration.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2260 
2261   // Notify ACDS of host loss (lightweight notification, no NAT data exchanged!)
2262   acip_host_lost_t host_lost = {0};
2263   memcpy(host_lost.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2264   memcpy(host_lost.participant_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2265   memcpy(host_lost.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2266   host_lost.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2267   host_lost.disconnect_time_ms = session->migration.detection_time_ms;
2268 
2269   packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_LOST, &host_lost, sizeof(host_lost), 0);
2270 
2271   // Check if we have a pre-elected future host (from last 5-minute ring round)
2272   if (session->ring.future_host_id[0] == 0) {
2273     // No future host known! This shouldn't happen if we just received FUTURE_HOST_ELECTED
2274     // within the last 5 minutes. Fall back to treating this as fatal.
2275     log_error("No future host pre-elected! Session cannot recover from host disconnect.");
2276     session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2277     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Session should end
2278   }
2279 
2280   // **SIMPLIFIED**: No metrics exchange needed! Future host was already elected 5 minutes ago.
2281   // Just failover to the pre-elected host.
2282   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER;
2283 
2284   // Check if I am the future host
2285   if (session->ring.am_future_host) {
2286     // I become the new host immediately! (no election delay!)
2287     log_info("Becoming host (I'm the pre-elected future host)");
2288     return discovery_session_become_host(session);
2289   } else {
2290     // Connect to pre-elected future host (address already stored!)
2291     log_info("Connecting to pre-elected future host: %s:%u", session->ring.future_host_address,
2292              session->ring.future_host_port);
2293     return discovery_session_connect_to_future_host(session);
2294   }
2295 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process
  (discovery_session_t * session, int64_t timeout_ns)


Process session events (call in main loop). Handles incoming ACDS
    messages, negotiation, and state transitions.




Parameters


Session Session context


timeout_ns Max time to wait for events in nanoseconds (0 =
  non-blocking)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error on failure


Definition at line 1416 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1416                                                                                               {
1417   if (!session) {
1418     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1419   }
1420 
1421   // Handle state-specific processing
1422   switch (session->state) {
1423   case DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER: {
1424     // Wait for PARTICIPANT_JOINED notification from ACDS
1425     // Note: don't use a hard timeout here - let the outer process timeout (snapshot delay) control max wait
1426     // The discovery_run() loop will keep calling this until session becomes active or process exits
1427     uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
1428     uint64_t wait_duration = now_ns - session->waiting_peer_start_ns;
1429 
1430     log_debug("★ WAITING_PEER: duration=%.3fs (no timeout, waiting for peer...)", wait_duration / 1e9);
1431 
1432     if (session->acds_transport && timeout_ns > 0) {
1433       // Do not set socket timeout - let receive_packet_secure use its own timeout
1434       // Setting a short socket timeout conflicts with its hardcoded 1-second timeout
1435       packet_type_t type;
1436       void *data = NULL;
1437       size_t len = 0;
1438       void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1439 
1440       // Try to receive a packet
1441       asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1442           packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1443       if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1444         if (type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_PARTICIPANT_JOINED) {
1445           log_info("Peer joined session, transitioning to negotiation");
1446           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING);
1447           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1448         } else {
1449           // Got some other packet, handle it or ignore
1450           log_debug("Received packet type %d while waiting for peer", type);
1451           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1452         }
1453       } else if (recv_result == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1454         // Timeout is normal, just return
1455       } else {
1456         log_debug("Packet receive error while waiting for peer: %d", recv_result);
1457       }
1458     } else {
1459       // Fallback to sleep if no timeout or invalid transport
1460       platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1461     }
1462     break;
1463   }
1464 
1465   case DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING: {
1466     // Handle NAT negotiation
1467     if (session->negotiate.state == NEGOTIATE_STATE_INIT) {
1468       // Initialize negotiation
1469       negotiate_init(&session->negotiate, session->session_id, session->participant_id, session->is_initiator);
1470       log_info("Starting NAT negotiation (initiator=%d)", session->is_initiator);
1471 
1472       // Send our NETWORK_QUALITY to ACDS so peers can evaluate us
1473       asciichat_error_t send_result = send_network_quality_to_acds(session);
1474       if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1475         log_warn("Failed to send initial NETWORK_QUALITY: %d", send_result);
1476       }
1477 
1478       // ACDS relays NETWORK_QUALITY only to OTHER participants, not back to sender
1479       // For now in discovery mode, assume we're the only one and become host immediately
1480       // A real implementation would wait for peer to join via PARTICIPANT_JOINED, then connect directly
1481       log_info("Proceeding as host (ACDS cannot relay peer quality in solo mode)");
1482       session->negotiate.our_quality.detection_complete = true;
1483       session->negotiate.peer_quality_received = true;
1484       memset(&session->negotiate.peer_quality, 0, sizeof(session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1485       // Mark peer quality as detected but with worst NAT type so we win host election
1486       session->negotiate.peer_quality.detection_complete = true;
1487       session->negotiate.peer_quality.nat_type = ACIP_NAT_TYPE_SYMMETRIC;
1488     }
1489 
1490     // Check for HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT from ACDS (in case we're waiting for host discovery)
1491     if (session->acds_transport && !session->is_initiator && !session->host_address[0]) {
1492       packet_type_t type;
1493       void *data = NULL;
1494       size_t len = 0;
1495       void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1496 
1497       asciichat_error_t recv_result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1498       if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK && type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT) {
1499         acip_host_announcement_t *announcement = (acip_host_announcement_t *)data;
1500         log_info("Received HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT from ACDS: %s:%u", announcement->host_address, announcement->host_port);
1501         SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, announcement->host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1502         session->host_port = announcement->host_port;
1503         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1504         // Continue to host role determination below
1505       } else if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1506         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1507       }
1508     }
1509 
1510     // Determine host role (now that peer_quality is set)
1511     if (session->negotiate.peer_quality_received && session->negotiate.state != NEGOTIATE_STATE_COMPARING) {
1512       log_info("Determining host role based on NAT qualities...");
1513       asciichat_error_t result = negotiate_determine_result(&session->negotiate);
1514       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1515         session->is_host = session->negotiate.we_are_host;
1516 
1517         if (session->is_host) {
1518           // We become the host
1519           log_info("🏠 NAT negotiation complete: WE ARE HOST (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1520                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1521                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1522           memcpy(session->host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
1523           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1524           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1525 
1526           // Fallback to localhost if NAT detection failed to determine address
1527           if (!session->host_address[0]) {
1528             log_warn("NAT address empty, using localhost fallback");
1529             SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(session->host_address));
1530           }
1531 
1532           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST);
1533         } else {
1534           // They are host (shouldn't happen in solo mode, but handle it)
1535           log_info("👤 NAT negotiation complete: THEY ARE HOST at %s:%u (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1536                    session->negotiate.host_address, session->negotiate.host_port,
1537                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1538                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1539           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1540           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1541 
1542           // If host address is empty, wait for HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT from ACDS instead of using fallback
1543           if (!session->host_address[0]) {
1544             log_info("Waiting for HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT from ACDS to discover host address...");
1545             // Stay in NEGOTIATING state to continue waiting for HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT
1546             break;
1547           }
1548 
1549           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST);
1550         }
1551       } else {
1552         log_error("Failed to determine negotiation result: %d", result);
1553         set_error(session, result, "Host election failed");
1554       }
1555     }
1556     break;
1557   }
1558 
1559   case DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST: {
1560     // Start hosting
1561     if (!session->host_ctx) {
1562       // Create host context with configured port
1563       int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
1564 
1565       session_host_config_t hconfig = {
1566           .port = host_port,
1567           .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
1568           .max_clients = 32,
1569           .encryption_enabled = true,
1570       };
1571 
1572       session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
1573       if (!session->host_ctx) {
1574         log_error("Failed to create host context");
1575         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create host context");
1576         break;
1577       }
1578 
1579       // Start listening
1580       asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
1581       if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1582         log_error("Failed to start host: %d", hstart);
1583         set_error(session, hstart, "Failed to start host");
1584         break;
1585       }
1586 
1587       log_info("Host started, listening for connections");
1588     }
1589 
1590     // Notify listeners that we're ready
1591     if (session->on_session_ready) {
1592       session->on_session_ready(session->session_string, session->callback_user_data);
1593     }
1594 
1595     // Send HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS so participants can discover us
1596     int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
1597     acip_host_announcement_t announcement = {0};
1598     memcpy(announcement.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
1599     memcpy(announcement.host_id, session->host_id, 16);
1600     SAFE_STRNCPY(announcement.host_address, session->host_address, sizeof(announcement.host_address));
1601     announcement.host_port = host_port;
1602     announcement.connection_type = ACIP_CONNECTION_TYPE_DIRECT_PUBLIC;
1603 
1604     if (session->acds_transport) {
1605       packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT, &announcement,
1606                                 sizeof(announcement), 0);
1607       log_info("Sent HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS at %s:%u", announcement.host_address, announcement.host_port);
1608     }
1609 
1610     set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1611     break;
1612   }
1613 
1614   case DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST: {
1615     // Connect to host as participant
1616 
1617     // For WebRTC sessions, wait for DataChannel to open
1618     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
1619       // Check if transport is ready (DataChannel opened)
1620       if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
1621         log_info("WebRTC DataChannel established, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
1622         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1623         break;
1624       }
1625 
1626       // If not ready and we haven't initiated connection yet, initiate it
1627       if (session->peer_manager && !session->webrtc_connection_initiated) {
1628         log_info("Initiating WebRTC connection to host (attempt %d/%d)...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1629                  GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts) + 1);
1630 
1631         // Initiate connection (generates offer, triggers SDP exchange)
1632         asciichat_error_t conn_result =
1633             webrtc_peer_manager_connect(session->peer_manager, session->session_id, session->host_id);
1634 
1635         if (conn_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1636           log_error("Failed to initiate WebRTC connection: %d", conn_result);
1637           set_error(session, conn_result, "Failed to initiate WebRTC connection");
1638           break;
1639         }
1640 
1641         log_info("WebRTC connection initiated, waiting for DataChannel...");
1642         session->webrtc_connection_initiated = true;
1643         session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = APP_CALLBACK_UINT64(platform_get_monotonic_time_us) / 1000;
1644       }
1645 
1646       // Check for ICE gathering timeout on all peers
1647       if (session->webrtc_connection_initiated && session->peer_manager) {
1648         int timeout_ms = GET_OPTION(webrtc_ice_timeout_ms);
1649         int timed_out_count = webrtc_peer_manager_check_gathering_timeouts(session->peer_manager, timeout_ms);
1650 
1651         if (timed_out_count > 0) {
1652           log_error("ICE gathering timeout: %d peer(s) failed to gather candidates within %dms", timed_out_count,
1653                     timeout_ms);
1654 
1655           // Check if we have retries remaining
1656           int max_attempts = GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts);
1657           if (session->webrtc_retry_attempt < max_attempts) {
1658             // Calculate exponential backoff delay
1659             uint32_t backoff_ms = calculate_backoff_delay_ms(session->webrtc_retry_attempt);
1660 
1661             log_warn("WebRTC connection attempt %d/%d failed, retrying in %ums...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1662                      max_attempts + 1, backoff_ms);
1663 
1664             // Wait for backoff period
1665             APP_CALLBACK_VOID(platform_pump_events);
1666             APP_CALLBACK_VOID_UINT(platform_sleep_ms, backoff_ms);
1667 
1668             // Destroy old peer manager
1669             webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(session->peer_manager);
1670             session->peer_manager = NULL;
1671 
1672             // Re-initialize peer manager for retry
1673             asciichat_error_t reinit_result = initialize_webrtc_peer_manager(session);
1674             if (reinit_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1675               log_error("Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry: %d", reinit_result);
1676               set_error(session, reinit_result, "Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry");
1677 
1678               // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error
1679               if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1680                 log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1681                 set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1682                 return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1683               }
1684 
1685               break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1686             }
1687 
1688             // Reset connection state for retry
1689             session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
1690             session->webrtc_retry_attempt++;
1691 
1692             log_info("WebRTC peer manager re-initialized for attempt %d/%d", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1693                      max_attempts + 1);
1694           } else {
1695             // No more retries - connection failed
1696             log_error("WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (timeout: %dms)", max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1697 
1698             // Set error state - WebRTC connection failed
1699             char error_msg[256];
1700             snprintf(error_msg, sizeof(error_msg), "WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (%dms timeout)",
1701                      max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1702             set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, error_msg);
1703 
1704             // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error (no TCP fallback)
1705             if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1706               log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1707               set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1708               return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1709             }
1710 
1711             break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1712           }
1713         }
1714       }
1715 
1716       // Poll ACDS for WebRTC signaling messages (SDP answer, ICE candidates)
1717       // while waiting for DataChannel to open
1718       if (session->acds_transport && acip_transport_has_pending_data(session->acds_transport)) {
1719         packet_type_t type;
1720         void *data = NULL;
1721         size_t len = 0;
1722         void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1723 
1724         asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1725             packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1726 
1727         if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1728           // Dispatch to appropriate handler
1729           handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
1730           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1731         } else {
1732           log_debug("Failed to receive ACDS packet: %d", recv_result);
1733         }
1734       }
1735 
1736       // Stay in CONNECTING_HOST until DataChannel opens (webrtc_transport_ready becomes true)
1737       // The discovery_on_transport_ready callback will set webrtc_transport_ready=true
1738       break;
1739     }
1740 
1741     if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1742       // Create participant context for this session
1743       session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
1744           .address = session->host_address,
1745           .port = session->host_port,
1746           .enable_audio = true,
1747           .enable_video = true,
1748           .encryption_enabled = true,
1749       };
1750 
1751       session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);
1752       if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1753         log_error("Failed to create participant context");
1754         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create participant context");
1755         break;
1756       }
1757 
1758       // Attempt connection
1759       asciichat_error_t pconn = session_participant_connect(session->participant_ctx);
1760       if (pconn != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1761         log_error("Failed to connect as participant: %d", pconn);
1762         // Stay in this state, will retry on next process() call
1763         break;
1764       }
1765 
1766       log_info("Connected to host, performing crypto handshake...");
1767 
1768       // Perform crypto handshake with server
1769       // Get the socket from participant context
1770       socket_t participant_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
1771       if (participant_socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1772         log_error("Failed to get socket from participant context");
1773         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to get participant socket");
1774         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1775         break;
1776       }
1777 
1778       // Step 1: Send client protocol version
1779       log_debug("Sending protocol version to server...");
1780       protocol_version_packet_t client_version = {0};
1781       client_version.protocol_version = HOST_TO_NET_U16(1);  // Protocol version 1
1782       client_version.protocol_revision = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0); // Revision 0
1783       client_version.supports_encryption = 1;                // We support encryption
1784       client_version.compression_algorithms = 0;
1785       client_version.compression_threshold = 0;
1786       client_version.feature_flags = 0;
1787 
1788       int result = send_protocol_version_packet(participant_socket, &client_version);
1789       if (result != 0) {
1790         log_error("Failed to send protocol version to server");
1791         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send protocol version");
1792         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1793         break;
1794       }
1795 
1796       // Step 2: Receive server protocol version
1797       log_debug("Receiving server protocol version...");
1798       packet_type_t packet_type;
1799       void *payload = NULL;
1800       size_t payload_len = 0;
1801 
1802       int recv_result = receive_packet(participant_socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len);
1803       if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK || packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
1804         log_error("Failed to receive server protocol version (got type 0x%x)", packet_type);
1805         if (payload) {
1806           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1807         }
1808         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to receive protocol version from server");
1809         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1810         break;
1811       }
1812 
1813       if (payload_len != sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t)) {
1814         log_error("Invalid protocol version packet size: %zu", payload_len);
1815         buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1816         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Invalid protocol version packet");
1817         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1818         break;
1819       }
1820 
1821       protocol_version_packet_t server_version;
1822       memcpy(&server_version, payload, sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
1823       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1824 
1825       if (!server_version.supports_encryption) {
1826         log_error("Server does not support encryption");
1827         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Server does not support encryption");
1828         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1829         break;
1830       }
1831 
1832       log_info("Server protocol version: %u.%u (encryption: yes)", NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_version),
1833                NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_revision));
1834 
1835       // Step 3: Send crypto capabilities
1836       log_debug("Sending crypto capabilities...");
1837       crypto_capabilities_packet_t client_caps = {0};
1838       client_caps.supported_kex_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001);    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
1839       client_caps.supported_auth_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0003);   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519 | AUTH_ALGO_NONE
1840       client_caps.supported_cipher_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001); // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
1841       client_caps.requires_verification = 0;
1842       client_caps.preferred_kex = 0x0001;    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
1843       client_caps.preferred_auth = 0x0001;   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519
1844       client_caps.preferred_cipher = 0x0001; // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
1845 
1846       result = send_crypto_capabilities_packet(participant_socket, &client_caps);
1847       if (result != 0) {
1848         log_error("Failed to send crypto capabilities");
1849         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send crypto capabilities");
1850         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1851         break;
1852       }
1853 
1854       log_info("Crypto handshake initiated successfully");
1855       log_warn("*** Connected to host as participant - transitioning to ACTIVE ***");
1856     }
1857 
1858     // Check if connection is established
1859     bool ctx_valid = session->participant_ctx != NULL;
1860     bool is_connected = ctx_valid ? session_participant_is_connected(session->participant_ctx) : false;
1861     log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - participant_ctx=%p, ctx_valid=%d, is_connected=%d",
1862               session->participant_ctx, ctx_valid, is_connected);
1863 
1864     if (ctx_valid && !is_connected) {
1865       // Log more details about why it's not connected
1866       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - context exists but not connected yet");
1867     }
1868 
1869     if (is_connected) {
1870       log_info("Participant connection confirmed, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
1871       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1872     } else {
1873       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - awaiting connection establishment (ctx_valid=%d, "
1874                 "is_connected=%d)",
1875                 ctx_valid, is_connected);
1876     }
1877     break;
1878   }
1879 
1880   case DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE:
1881     // Receive and handle ACDS packets for WebRTC signaling (NEW)
1882     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC && session->acds_transport &&
1883         acip_transport_has_pending_data(session->acds_transport)) {
1884       packet_type_t type;
1885       void *data = NULL;
1886       size_t len = 0;
1887       void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1888 
1889       asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1890           packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1891 
1892       if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1893         // Dispatch to appropriate handler
1894         handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
1895         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1896       }
1897     }
1898 
1899     // Session is active - check for host disconnect
1900     if (!session->is_host) {
1901       // We are a participant - check if host is still alive
1902       asciichat_error_t host_check = discovery_session_check_host_alive(session);
1903       if (host_check != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1904         // Host is down! Trigger automatic failover to pre-elected future host
1905         log_warn("Host disconnect detected during ACTIVE state");
1906 
1907         // If --prefer-webrtc is set, WebRTC failure is fatal (no TCP fallback)
1908         const options_t *opts = options_get();
1909         if (opts && opts->prefer_webrtc) {
1910           log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1911           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1912           session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
1913           return ERROR_NETWORK;
1914         }
1915 
1916         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING);
1917         // Reason code: 1 = timeout/disconnect detected during ACTIVE session
1918         discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect(session, 1);
1919       }
1920     }
1921     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1922     break;
1923 
1924   case DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING:
1925     // Host migration in progress
1926     // Check if migration has timed out (if it takes >30 seconds, give up)
1927     if (session->migration.state == MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE) {
1928       // Migration completed successfully
1929       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1930       log_info("Migration complete, session resumed");
1931     } else if (session_get_current_time_ms() - session->migration.detection_time_ms > 30 * MS_PER_SEC_INT) {
1932       // Migration timed out after 30 seconds
1933       log_error("Host migration timeout - session cannot recover");
1934       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1935       session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
1936     }
1937     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1938     break;
1939 
1940   case DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED:
1941     // Session has failed - return error on further processing
1942     return session->error != ASCIICHAT_OK ? session->error : ERROR_NETWORK;
1943 
1944   case DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED:
1945     // Session has ended gracefully - return OK
1946     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1947 
1948   case DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT:
1949   case DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_ACDS:
1950   case DISCOVERY_STATE_CREATING_SESSION:
1951   case DISCOVERY_STATE_JOINING_SESSION:
1952     // These states are handled during discovery_session_start(), not in the process loop
1953     // If we reach here, something went wrong - sleep and wait
1954     log_debug("discovery_session_process: State %d should be handled by discovery_session_start", session->state);
1955     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1956     break;
1957 
1958   default:
1959     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid session state: %d", session->state);
1960   }
1961 
1962   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1963 }






References discovery_session_t::acds_transport,
    ACIP_CONNECTION_TYPE_DIRECT_PUBLIC, acip_host_announcement_t,
    ACIP_NAT_TYPE_SYMMETRIC, APP_CALLBACK_UINT64,

    discovery_session_t::migration, MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE,
    MS_PER_SEC_INT, nat_compute_tier(),
    nat_quality_t::nat_type, discovery_session_t::negotiate,
    negotiate_determine_result(), negotiate_init(),
    NEGOTIATE_STATE_COMPARING, NEGOTIATE_STATE_INIT,
    NET_TO_HOST_U16, discovery_session_t::on_session_ready,
    options_get(), negotiate_ctx_t::our_quality,
    packet_receive_via_transport(), packet_send_via_transport(),
    PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT,
    PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_PARTICIPANT_JOINED,
    PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION,
    discovery_session_t::participant_ctx,
    discovery_session_t::participant_id,
    discovery_session_t::peer_manager,
    negotiate_ctx_t::peer_quality,
    negotiate_ctx_t::peer_quality_received,
    platform_get_monotonic_time_us(), platform_sleep_ms(),
    platform_sleep_us(), options_state::prefer_webrtc,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::preferred_auth,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::preferred_cipher,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::preferred_kex,

    protocol_version_packet_t::protocol_version, receive_packet(),

    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::supported_cipher_algorithms,

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Start the discovery session. Connects to ACDS and either creates
    or joins a session.




Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Definition at line 1227 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1227                                                                         {
1228   log_info("discovery_session_start: ENTRY - session=%p", session);
1229 
1230   if (!session) {
1231     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1232   }
1233 
1234   log_info("discovery_session_start: is_initiator=%d, session_string='%s'", session->is_initiator,
1235            session->session_string);
1236 
1237   // Check if we should exit before starting blocking operations
1238   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1239     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (before ACDS connect)");
1240     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1241   }
1242 
1243   log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling connect_to_acds...");
1244   // Connect to ACDS
1245   asciichat_error_t result = connect_to_acds(session);
1246   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1247     log_error("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds FAILED - result=%d", result);
1248     set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1249     return result;
1250   }
1251   log_info("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds succeeded");
1252 
1253   // Check again after connection
1254   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1255     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after ACDS connect)");
1256     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1257   }
1258 
1259   // Perform crypto handshake with ACDS (same as client-server handshake)
1260   log_info("discovery_session_start: Performing crypto handshake with ACDS...");
1261   crypto_handshake_context_t handshake_ctx;
1262   memset(&handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(handshake_ctx));
1263 
1264   result = crypto_handshake_init("discovery_client", &handshake_ctx, false);
1265   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1266     log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake init failed - %s", asciichat_error_string(result));
1267     set_error(session, result, "Crypto handshake init failed");
1268     return result;
1269   }
1270 
1271   // Store identity key in handshake context
1272   handshake_ctx.client_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1273   handshake_ctx.client_private_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1274   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_public_key.key, session->identity_pubkey, 32);
1275   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_private_key.key.ed25519, session->identity_seckey, 64);
1276 
1277   // Step 0: Send protocol version to ACDS
1278   log_debug("Sending protocol version to ACDS...");
1279   protocol_version_packet_t client_version = {0};
1280   client_version.protocol_version = HOST_TO_NET_U16(1);
1281   client_version.protocol_revision = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0);
1282   client_version.supports_encryption = 1;
1283   client_version.compression_algorithms = 0;
1284   client_version.compression_threshold = 0;
1285   client_version.feature_flags = 0;
1286 
1287   result = packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION, &client_version,
1288                                      sizeof(client_version), 0);
1289   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1290     log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to send protocol version to ACDS");
1291     set_error(session, result, "Failed to send protocol version to ACDS");
1292     return result;
1293   }
1294 
1295   // Step 0.5: Receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS from ACDS
1296   {
1297     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1298     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1299     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1300     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1301 
1302     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1303     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK || pkt_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_PARAMETERS) {
1304       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS (got type %u)", pkt_type);
1305       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1306       set_error(session, result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? result : ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
1307                 "Failed to receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS");
1308       return result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? result : ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL;
1309     }
1310 
1311     if (pkt_len != sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t)) {
1312       log_error("discovery_session_start: Invalid CRYPTO_PARAMETERS size: %zu (expected %zu)", pkt_len,
1313                 sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
1314       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1315       set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid CRYPTO_PARAMETERS size");
1316       return ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL;
1317     }
1318 
1319     crypto_parameters_packet_t server_params;
1320     memcpy(&server_params, pkt_data, sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
1321     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1322 
1323     // Pass params in network byte order — crypto_handshake_set_parameters
1324     // handles conversion internally for client (is_server=false)
1325     result = crypto_handshake_set_parameters(&handshake_ctx, &server_params);
1326     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1327       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to set crypto parameters");
1328       set_error(session, result, "Failed to set crypto parameters");
1329       return result;
1330     }
1331     log_info("discovery_session_start: CRYPTO_PARAMETERS received and applied");
1332   }
1333 
1334   // Step 1: Key exchange - receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT and respond
1335   {
1336     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1337     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1338     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1339     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1340 
1341     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1342     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1343       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT");
1344       set_error(session, result, "Handshake key exchange recv failed");
1345       return result;
1346     }
1347 
1348     result = crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1349     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1350 
1351     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1352       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake key exchange failed");
1353       set_error(session, result, "Handshake key exchange failed");
1354       return result;
1355     }
1356   }
1357 
1358   // Step 2: Receive CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE from server to finalize handshake
1359   {
1360     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1361     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1362     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1363     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1364 
1365     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1366     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1367       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE");
1368       set_error(session, result, "Failed to receive handshake complete");
1369       return result;
1370     }
1371 
1372     if (pkt_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE) {
1373       log_error("discovery_session_start: Expected CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE, got packet type %d", pkt_type);
1374       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1375       set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid crypto handshake completion packet");
1376       return ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL;
1377     }
1378 
1379     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1380   }
1381 
1382   // For ACDS, handshake completes after key exchange (no auth required)
1383   log_info("discovery_session_start: ACDS handshake complete");
1384   log_info("discovery_session_start: Crypto handshake with ACDS completed");
1385 
1386   // Check again after handshake
1387   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1388     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after handshake)");
1389     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1390   }
1391 
1392   // Create or join session
1393   if (session->is_initiator) {
1394     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling create_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1395     result = create_session(session);
1396     log_info("discovery_session_start: create_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, result=%d",
1397              session->participant_ctx, result);
1398 
1399     if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1400       // Initiator also needs to register itself as a participant so it can receive PARTICIPANT_JOINED notifications
1401       // when other peers join. This makes the initiator discoverable to other participants.
1402       log_info("Initiator: now registering self as participant in created session");
1403       result = join_session(session);
1404       log_info("Initiator: join_session result=%d", result);
1405     }
1406     return result;
1407   } else {
1408     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling join_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1409     asciichat_error_t result = join_session(session);
1410     log_info("discovery_session_start: join_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, state=%d, result=%d",
1411              session->participant_ctx, session->state, result);
1412     return result;
1413   }
1414 }

  discovery_session_stop (discovery_session_t * session)

Parameters


Session Session context

1965                                                           {
1966   if (!session) {
1967     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1968     return;
1969   }
1970 
1971   log_info("Stopping discovery session...");
1972 
1973   // Send SESSION_LEAVE if connected
1974   if (session->acds_transport && session->session_id[0] != 0) {
1975     acip_session_leave_t leave_msg;
1976     memset(&leave_msg, 0, sizeof(leave_msg));
1977     memcpy(leave_msg.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
1978     memcpy(leave_msg.participant_id, session->participant_id, 16);
1979 
1980     packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_LEAVE, &leave_msg, sizeof(leave_msg),
1981                               0);
1982   }
1983 
1984   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED);
1985 }
